title: SAC Jets Strike PDF Units in Myaing–Pauk Oil Field Area — Five Resistance Fighters Killed
detail: Myanmar junta fighter jets struck PDF (NUG-aligned) resistance units operating in an oil field area between Myaing and Pauk townships in Magway Region on April 4, killing five fighters and injuring others. The PDF had surrounded oil fields in the area controlled by the SAC's Defense Minister for the Magway Federal Unit, triggering an air response. The strike reflected the junta's increasing reliance on airpower to defend economic assets and break resistance encirclements in Magway Region. The Myaing-Pauk oil fields are part of Myanmar's onshore petroleum infrastructure in Magway; junta control over these assets generates revenue critical to sustaining military operations.

title: Junta Troops Fire Indiscriminately in Mudon Township, Mon State — Civilian Truck Worker Killed
detail: Clashes between junta troops and resistance forces broke out around 5:00 PM on April 4 in Mudon Township, Mon State. Junta soldiers fired indiscriminately during the engagement, killing a 35-year-old truck assistant who was traveling with a consignment of fish paste. The death reflected the junta's documented pattern of collateral civilian casualties during counter-insurgency operations in Mon State, where resistance groups have expanded operations since 2022. Mudon Township borders Karen State and has seen periodic clashes between SAC forces and resistance units crossing the administrative boundary.
